rulings.Case Title: THEODORE BROWN & PARTNERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DINESH MANIAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Written Discovery And Awarding Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2012, Line1. DEFENDANT FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Written Discovery And Awarding Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted as unopposed. Counsel for moving party to provide an appropriate order to the court for execution on May 15, 2012 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXA RUDIN et al VS. HILL & COMPANY REAL ESTATE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Mtn For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT ELIZABETH FETTER Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion For Good Faith Settlement. Granted. The motion is treated as an invitation to court to invoke its authority to reconsider the prior order on the court's own motion and the motion is granted on that basis, not based on the reconsideration statute. The portion of the order dismissing all of Ms. Fetter's claims against Hill & Company and its agents was made largely on the ill-considered, but not constituting a judicial admission, statement of Mr. Zacks at the April 24 hearing, which is contrary to the statement he made at the April 20 hearing. The prior order is vacated and replaced with a new order tracking the language of the tentative ruling issued in advance of the April 20 hearing and leaving open the issue of what claims, if any, of Ms. Fetter remain notwithstanding the confirmation that the settlement was made in good faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IRENE GUEVARA VS. E.A.H., INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue To Marin County
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT JOHN HUNTER, EAH, INC. (SUED HEREIN AS E.A.H. INC., Motion To Transfer Venue To Marin County. Denied. Venue is proper in San Francisco pursuant to Government Code section 12965(b) and the portion of the motion based on convenience of the witnesses is premature. Judge Kahn discloses that Steven Werth, one of the attorneys for defendant E.A.H., coached his son's little leaguer team last year and that Judge Kahn, while he was an associate with Heller, Ehrman, White & McAuliffe in 1983-87, represented the Ecumenical Association for Housing, but that neither of these facts affect his ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL F. KLAPPER VS. GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED, AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Relief From Privilege Waiver And Order Compelling Production Of Privileged Documents; Memo Of P & A And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2012, Line 5. DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED'S Motion For Relief From Privilege Waiver And Order Compelling Production Of Privileged Documents. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is denied; the court is without power to reconsiders and change the ruling previously entered. Even if the court did have such power, there is no showing that would justify a different ruling. Plaintiff is awarded sanctions in the amount of $ 3,040.00 against defendant Gentry and its attorneys. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CASSAUNDRA ELLENA VS. STANDARD INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANT THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE, THE COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF, DAVID JONES DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not alleged that the Insurance Commissioner violated a specific mandatory duty. A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions involving the Commissioner's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the Commissioner abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MING ZHU, LLC VS. SAN FRANCISCO MEDICAL IMAGING, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Set Aside Court'S Order Of 3/27/12
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANT FLORA IRA Motion To Set Aside Court'S Order Of 3/27/12. Denied without prejudice to being re-filed. The moving party did not provide courtesy copies of the motion to department 302 as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Moreover, the court's records do not show that any order was issued or filed on March 27, 2012 and it appears that the motion may need to be heard in department 610, not department 302. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JERRY PARRIS et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Pltfs' Counsel And Counsel'S Law Firm
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 15, 2012, Line 24, DEFENDANT SHIRA SHAVIT Motion To Disqualify Plaintiffs' Counsel And Counsel'S Law Firm. Denied. Dr. Shavit has not shown that there is a substantial relationship between the O'Connell action and this case. Moreover, the undisputed evidence shows that counsel for plaintiff was not privy to any information from the O'Connell action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
